subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Shared Responsibility Model
description: A 60-second lesson on The Shared Responsibility Model.

hook.text: A company's database is exposed to the internet. Whose fault? The cloud provider's or the customer's?

buildup.text: Cloud security is split: the provider secures the infrastructure, the customer secures what runs on it.

discovery.text: AWS calls it 'security of the cloud vs. security in the cloud.' The line varies by service model.

twist.text: Over 90% of cloud security failures are the customer's fault. Misconfiguration is the top cause.

climax.text: Understanding where your responsibility starts is the single most important cloud security concept.

punchline.text: The provider secures the cloud. You secure what's in it.

quiz.question: What causes over 90% of cloud security failures?
options: Customer misconfiguration, not cloud provider vulnerabilities
Cloud providers using outdated encryption methods
